anti_cheat: Held-out behavioral probes (1799/1800/899) imported from the candidate session.mjs + checkout.mjs prove the new TTL flows through the REAL module graph, not a hardcoded sibling or the trace report; config-content must literally show 1800 and not 900.
notes: Gauntlet Layer 2 (operator suggestedtests.md) — the multi-module cross-cutting trace / LONG-CONTEXT retention + peripheral-preservation test. The fixture is a minimal nested monorepo (all ESM .mjs, no build step): a single source constant TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS=900 in packages/auth/config.mjs flows through session.isExpired into gateway.canCheckout. The agent must (1) raise the TTL 900->1800 AT THE SOURCE ONLY, (2) write trace-report.json at the workspace root — an ordered JSON array of {file,symbol,role} hops from the source through every runtime-gating consumer, and (3) leave two load-bearing peripheral modules (packages/core/session-sync.mjs, packages/db-migrator/schema.mjs) byte-identical — the long-context trap is that they are easy to collaterally damage while editing. entrypoint=trace-report.json is the deliverable run-suite checks exists before invoking the oracle; the workspace root is found via --workdir. Three weighted groups: patch(.35) verifies config literally shows 1800 (not 900) AND probes HELD-OUT timestamps (1799/1800/899) by importing the candidate session.mjs+checkout.mjs via pathToFileURL — held-out values kill a hardcoded TTL and prove the value flows through the live graph; integrity(.35, GATING) requires both peripherals byte-identical and undeleted, a failed gate => gated_fail ['integrity'] and score 0 (collateral damage is not partially shippable); trace(.30) requires trace-report.json to parse as a JSON array naming the source (config.mjs), the session hop (session.mjs/isExpired) and the gateway hop (checkout.mjs/canCheckout). The oracle reads the original peripheral bytes from its own fixtures/ for the byte-identical compare. validate.mjs proves: reference -> 1.0 (no gated_fail, no dq); editing session-sync.mjs or deleting db-migrator/schema.mjs -> gated_fail ['integrity'] score 0; reverting config to 900 -> patch<1 score<1 (not gated); hardcoding 1800 in session.mjs while config stays 900 -> patch<1 (config-content fails); removing trace-report.json -> trace 0; a trace-report missing the gateway hop -> trace<1.
